name: documents
notes: One row inserted per upload: name comes from req.body.name or the original filename; file_type is the MIME type string; file_path is /uploads/{uuid}{ext}; tags is a PostgreSQL TEXT[] parsed from a comma-delimited request body field (null when absent); uploaded_by is req.user.email (null when unauthenticated under DEV_SKIP_AUTH).
After INSERT, the PostgreSQL trigger trg_sync_to_v2 fires synchronously and copies the row into documents_v2 without the tags column.

name: documents_v2
notes: Never written directly by the upload route — populated exclusively via the trg_sync_to_v2 AFTER INSERT trigger on the documents table.
The trigger deliberately omits the tags column, so documents_v2.tags is always NULL for every document uploaded through this flow — a known bug in the legacy implementation.
